title: Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Mizoram

overview.short: Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Saitual district in eastern Mizoram, northeast India.
overview.description: Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area in Saitual district in eastern Mizoram, northeast India. It is an alpine forest and contains the second highest peak in Mizoram. It is specially a conservation interest on rare species of birds. It was declared a protected area in 1999, and a national wildlife sanctuary by the Indian Ministry of Environment and Forests on 31 May 2001.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lengpui Airport, Aizawl (61 km) and the nearest railway station is Agartala Railway Station (199 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lengteng Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Lamzawl, Ngopa, Selam, Champhai, Kawlbem.
